title: Escrow Deposit Requirements and Compliance for Licensed Recruitment Agencies
content:
SECTION 17. Maintenance of Escrow Deposit. A licensed recruitment agency must maintain an escrow deposit of at least One Million Pesos (PhP1,000,000.00) at all times. If additional escrow is required pursuant to Section 15, the agency must maintain the base amount plus the additional escrow. If the escrow deposit is garnished, the agency must replenish it within 15 days of receiving a Notice to Replenish Escrow Deposit. Failure to comply results in license suspension, but the agency may explain within the same 15-day period to avoid suspension. The Administration ensures compliance with these escrow deposit requirements.

SECTION 18. Monitoring Compliance. The Administration monitors licensed recruitment agencies for compliance with licensing conditions, recruitment laws, rules on license use, labor standards, and occupational safety and health standards, coordinating with the DOLE Regional Office. The Administration may suspend or revoke licenses for failure to maintain required qualifications or conditions.

SECTION 19. Release of Deposit in Escrow. The escrow deposit of a licensed recruitment agency that voluntarily surrenders or does not renew its license will be released upon submission of: (a) a copy of the duly executed/signed escrow agreement; (b) bank certification on escrow deposit status; (c) Certificate of No Pending Case from the NLRC; (d) Clearance from the Adjudication Office; (e) Notarized Board or Partnership Resolution received by the SEC authorizing the withdrawal and naming the person to process and receive the escrow deposit check; and (f) a surety bond equal to the escrow deposit amount valid for four years from license expiration, if applicable.
